g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about Chi-Square Distribution and Goodness of Fit Test for statistical inference.
g.text: Chi-Square Distribution: Goodness of Fit Test
Module 4 - Statistical Inference for Computer Science

g.data-narration: Statistical inference helps draw conclusions about populations from sample data using hypothesis testing.
g.text: Statistical Inference helps draw conclusions about populations from sample data.
Chi-Square test checks if observed data matches expected distribution.
Used in ML model evaluation, A/B testing, quality control, and data analysis.

g.data-narration: Learn the five core concepts: Chi-Square distribution, Goodness of Fit test, test statistic, degrees of freedom, and decision rule.
The Chi-Square distribution is skewed right for small degrees of freedom and becomes more symmetric as df increases.
g.text: A continuous probability distribution for positive values, characterized by degrees of freedom.

g.data-narration: The null hypothesis states data follows the expected distribution, while the alternative states it does not.

g.text: The observed data FOLLOWS the hypothesized distribution
The observed data DOES NOT follow the hypothesized distribution

g.data-narration: The chi-square test statistic sums the squared differences between observed and expected frequencies divided by expected.
Degrees of freedom equals number of categories minus one minus estimated parameters. For a fair die test, df equals 5.
To decide: calculate chi-square, choose significance level, find critical value, then compare to make your decision.

g.text: If Chi-Square calc less/equal critical - Fail to Reject H0
If Chi-Square calc greater than critical - Reject H0

g.data-narration: Example: Testing if a die is fair by rolling it 60 times. Each face should appear 10 times if the die is fair.
g.text: Example: Testing a Fair Die (60 rolls)

g.data-narration: Computing the test statistic: sum of (observed minus expected) squared divided by expected equals 1.0.
Since calculated chi-square (1.0) is less than critical value (11.07), we fail to reject the null hypothesis.

g.text: No evidence the die is unfair!
